1949_color_scans.description: High-resolution color scans from 1949 printing of The Little Engine That Could (same Lois Lenski illustrations as 1930 first edition)

files.content: Title page illustration — blue engine pulling train over mountain with toy animals
Shiny black passenger engine passing by toy train on adjacent track
Small blue engine silhouette vignette with smoke rings
Big black engine at station, toys pleading on tracks
Enormous red passenger engine looming, toy train tiny behind
B&W: Large freight engine departing, toy train with animals below
B&W: Little engine looking up at mountain and departing big engine
Yellow/old engine with roundhouse behind, approaching slowly
Green engine with sad face departing, clown with flag on tracks
B&W: Big freight engine on upper track, toy train on lower track
Blue engine coupled to toy train, starting up mountain — the climb begins
B&W: Two trains on parallel tracks, small engine approaching
Blue engine pulling train up steep mountain slope — the iconic climb image
B&W: Train going down other side of mountain, engine smiling
Endpiece vignette: small black train silhouette

1930_bw_crops.description: Cropped illustrations from 1930 first edition B&W scan

files.content: Wide establishing shot with train and landscape
Shiny engine passing, toy train cars visible (color version)
Stopped engine with toys pleading (color version)
Engine straining up steepest part of mountain
Engine cresting the mountain ridge
Little engine approaching toy train, moment of decision
Train arriving, celebration

cover.description: 1st edition cover photograph from eBay listing

files.content: Red cloth cover with blue line art of train
